A11y Cookbook

  • Overview
    • How to test ?
        • Button
        • Link
        • Heading
        • Image
        • Table
        • List
        • Playground
        • Label
        • Input
        • Textarea
        • Content Editable
        • Checkbox
        • Radio Group
        • Switch
        • aria-label
        • aria-labelledby
        • aria-describedby
        • aria-expanded
        • aria-current
        • aria-live
        • Modal
        • Accordion
        • Tabs

      aria-current

      aria-current indicates which item represents the current item within a set.

      It is most commonly used in:

      • Navigation menus
      • Sidebars
      • Breadcrumbs
      • Pagination
      • Step indicators

      This helps screen reader users understand their current location.

      Example

      • Button
      • Input
      • Checkbox
      export function AriaCurrent() {
  return (
    <nav aria-label="Documentation">
      <ul className="space-y-2">
        <li>
          <a href="/button">Button</a>
        </li>

        <li>
          <a href="/input" aria-current="page">
            Input
          </a>
        </li>

        <li>
          <a href="/checkbox">Checkbox</a>
        </li>
      </ul>
    </nav>
  );
}

      Accessibility Notes

      • Applied only to the current item.
      • Most commonly uses aria-current="page".
      • Screen readers announce the current state.
      • Only one item in a set should typically be marked as current.

      Current Page

      <nav aria-label="Documentation">
  <ul>
    <li>
      <a href="/button"> Button </a>
    </li>

    <li>
      <a href="/input" aria-current="page"> Input </a>
    </li>

    <li>
      <a href="/checkbox"> Checkbox </a>
    </li>
  </ul>
</nav>

      Screen reader:

      Button link

Input link
current page

Checkbox link

      Sidebar Navigation

      A common pattern in documentation websites.

      <a href="/button"> Button </a>

<a href="/input" aria-current="page"> Input </a>

<a href="/checkbox"> Checkbox </a>

      Screen reader:

      Input
current page
link

      Users immediately know where they are.

      Breadcrumbs

      <nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">
  <ol>
    <li>
      <a href="/"> Home </a>
    </li>

    <li>
      <a href="/forms"> Forms </a>
    </li>

    <li>
      <a href="/forms/input" aria-current="page"> Input </a>
    </li>
  </ol>
</nav>

      Screen reader:

      Input
current page
link

      Pagination

      <nav aria-label="Pagination">
  <a href="?page=1"> 1 </a>

  <a href="?page=2" aria-current="page"> 2 </a>

  <a href="?page=3"> 3 </a>
</nav>

      Screen reader:

      2
current page
link

      Step Indicator

      <ol>
  <li>Account</li>

  <li aria-current="step">Profile</li>

  <li>Confirmation</li>
</ol>

      Screen reader:

      Profile
current step

      Supported Values

      Current Page

      aria-current="page"

      Used for:

      • Navigation menus
      • Sidebars
      • Breadcrumbs

      Current Step

      aria-current="step"

      Used for:

      • Multi-step forms
      • Checkout flows
      • Wizards

      Current Location

      aria-current="location"

      Used when indicating the current location within a structure.

      Boolean Form

      aria-current="true"

      Valid, but less descriptive.

      Prefer specific values such as:

      aria-current="page"

      or

      aria-current="step"

      when possible.

      aria-current vs aria-selected

      These attributes are often confused.

      aria-current

      Represents:

      Where am I?

      Example:

      <a href="/input" aria-current="page"> Input </a>

      Screen reader:

      current page

      aria-selected

      Represents:

      Which option is selected?

      Example:

      <button role="tab" aria-selected="true">Details</button>

      Screen reader:

      selected

      Use:

      • aria-current for navigation and location
      • aria-selected for selection widgets such as tabs, listboxes, and grids

      Common Mistakes

      ❌ Marking Multiple Pages as Current

      <a href="/button" aria-current="page"> Button </a>

<a href="/input" aria-current="page"> Input </a>

      Users cannot determine which page is actually current.

      ✅ Mark Only One Current Page

      <a href="/button"> Button </a>

<a href="/input" aria-current="page"> Input </a>

      ❌ Using Only Visual Styling

      <a class="active"> Input </a>

      Sighted users see the active state, but screen readers receive no indication.

      ✅ Combine Styling with aria-current

      <a class="active" aria-current="page"> Input </a>

      When to Use

      Good candidates:

      • Documentation sidebars
      • Site navigation
      • Breadcrumbs
      • Pagination
      • Multi-step forms
      • Checkout progress indicators

      Use aria-current whenever users need to know:

      Where am I right now?
